title: NME Sequel Prequel Pfft try a Midquel
display_title: NME Sequel Prequel Pfft try a Midquel
wikitext:
[]: NME: Sequel? Prequel? Pfft, try a Midquel.             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Aug 19, 2010 10:44 pm I figured separate theories call for separate threads instead of making things too messy, so yeah. Although I'm one of those weirdos who'd prefer an "Endless Eight"/loop scenario to something like Sequel Theory, this thought entered my brain somewhat recently and now I can't stop thinking about it. I guess it's something of an offshoot of Sequel Theory but not exactly the same thing. Anyway: oOoOoOo wrote: Rebuild is Asuka's dream. She's having it while being mindfucked in the original NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Thu Aug 19, 2010 10:47 pm No. Bad SSD.

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Thu Aug 19, 2010 10:47 pm SSD, you suck.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Thu Aug 19, 2010 10:53 pm a strange concept/theory but so is the "Mari is Asuka Soryu in disguise" one, so go figure

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Thu Aug 19, 2010 10:55 pm I'm not sure if O-san's quote is the best one to associate with this, as Asuka isn't in 1.xx. If Rebuild is anything other than an entirely new story, I suspect Kaworu will infer or support evidence in the next film(s).

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Thu Aug 19, 2010 11:25 pm HAHAHAHAHAHHAAHA Basically, my hilarious theory is that Rebuild is more "shonen" because that's what Asuka wanted in NGE. The world of Rebuild is warped by her expectations. They could play with this now that she's contaminated. I've no idea . It seems the qualities Asuka (in NGE) disliked in Shinji and Rei are less prominent in Rebuild. Asuka also seems to have a wild shift into mentally stable towards the end of the second Rebuild film. This is batshit insane postmodern/metafictional stuff. Whatever the fuck that means. It can also not be proven whatsoever. But the whole of Rebuild, to me, seems to require having seen NGE to enjoy. Like, I think Anno-san is specifically playing with those expectations. So I wouldn't be surprised if it's some crazy-ass shit like IT ALL TAKES PLACE DURING NGE'S INSTRUMENTALITY . Whether or not there is a canonical sequelness, I think Rebuild relies on NGE. It's so crazy. Let it flow. Good times, people, good times . And yes, Kaworu would help. Hurry up and explain yourself, you delicious demigod.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Aug 20, 2010 12:07 am so what i'm getting is that the theory essentially renders Rebuild into being Asuka's "Re-Take" hmmmm....

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Aug 20, 2010 12:13 am GasmaskAvenger wrote: so what i'm getting is that the theory essentially renders Rebuild into being Asuka's "Re-Take."

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Fri Aug 20, 2010 12:37 am You're wrong, SSD. It's obviously taking place while Shinji's stuck inside Unit-01. Being damn well close to God, Unit-01 can see all potential timelines. Shinji just happened to find himself believing he existed in this timeline. Considering that he gets a little badass during the Zeruel fight, it's no wonder that he's suddenly somewhat competent in Rebuild. Wrap your brain around that. >=3 (PS: my post ended in visual parallelism. Hope you caught it, everyone.)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Aug 20, 2010 12:40 am Any " ____ because of ____ " idea is fair game, I'd say.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Aug 20, 2010 12:48 am taking everything that has happened prior (the series, EoE, 1.0 and 2.0), the whole idea of Rebuild being all the creation of Asuka's mind during a mindfuck or Instrumentality (ala how Re-Take itself is all the creation of Shinji during whatever state he is in during the fic. I never read it, per say) who ultimately add on and compliment to her overall character arc in the original series it could also cement that if, at the end of "Rebuild Final", she finally matures, grows up and accepts who she is and what her purpose in life is, completing ditching her "Trojan Bitch" aspects completely and moving on in life if that does, in fact, happens, it's safe for me to say I can finally respect and like the character SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Fri Aug 20, 2010 12:54 am GasmaskAvenger wrote: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Aug 20, 2010 12:55 am okay then :)

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Fri Aug 20, 2010 12:59 am she finally matures, grows up and accepts who she is and what her purpose in life is

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Fri Aug 20, 2010 1:01 am heh just throwing food for thought out there

[]:             Quote Post by andrew050 » Fri Aug 20, 2010 4:58 am Well working off from this idea, what if it's Misato's mindfuck instead; seems like it could be her slightly more ideal world, she continues to fight the angels thus having purpose, and Shinji and Asuka seem more adjusted/do things after their little pep talks with her, which happen throughout both films. Also Rei is even better adjusted, and while it seems like she has nothing to do with Misato, the relationship of Rei and Shinji is important to Misato because of her want to "fix" Shinji. Then there's the (previously discussed) similarities of the scar on Lilith in Rebuild, and Misato's from the original. Now with some leaping in logic and connections, maybe "the lilith" is the (sorta) physical manifestation of Misato in her own mindfuck world, leading up to Misato reuniting with that figure that is "lilith" in the final films (much like Rei in EOE). and all this is totally open to mockery because I'm just throwing out the idea than saying it's plausible/fact...

[]:             Quote Post by CATO » Fri Aug 20, 2010 7:00 am Well working off from this idea, what if it's Misato's mindfuck instead

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Aug 20, 2010 8:35 am ^ I don't think this thread was ever meant to be taken seriously. Besides, it's obvious that it's Aoba's mindtrip. After getting out of the sea of LCL, he made it as a rock star (Third Impact or no, people still need rock music). He's living the S&D&R&R lifestyle and is currently hopped up on LSD. Mari is his subconscious female representation.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Fri Aug 20, 2010 9:13 am This theory is so bad a part of me died inside.

[]:             Quote Post by andrew050 » Fri Aug 20, 2010 10:11 am ran1 wrote: This theory is so bad a part of me died inside.
